code:
if (   # Example requires configured GitHub OAuth 2.0 app   # (go to https://github.com/settings/developers to create one):   nzchar(Sys.getenv("GITHUB_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID")) &&     nzchar(Sys.getenv("GITHUB_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET")) &&     interactive() ) {   library(shiny)   library(shinyOAuth)    # Define client   client <- oauth_client(     provider = oauth_provider_github(),     client_id = Sys.getenv("GITHUB_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID"),     client_secret = Sys.getenv("GITHUB_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET"),     redirect_uri = "http://127.0.0.1:8100"   )    # Choose which app you want to run   app_to_run <- NULL   while (!isTRUE(app_to_run %in% c(1:4))) {     app_to_run <- readline(       prompt = paste0(         "Which example app do you want to run?
",         "  1: Auto-redirect login
",         "  2: Manual login button
",         "  3: Fetch additional resource with access token
",         "  4: No app (all will be defined but none run)
",         "Enter 1, 2, 3, or 4... "       )     )   }    if (app_to_run %in% c(1:3)) {     cli::cli_alert_info(paste0(       "Will run example app {app_to_run} on {.url http://127.0.0.1:8100}
",       "Open this URL in a regular browser (viewers in RStudio/Positron/etc. ",       "cannot perform necessary redirects)"     ))   }    # Example app with auto-redirect (1) -----------------------------------------    ui_1 <- fluidPage(     use_shinyOAuth(),     uiOutput("login")   )    server_1 <- function(input, output, session) {     # Auto-redirect (default):     auth <- oauth_module_server(       "auth",       client,       auto_redirect = TRUE     )      output$login <- renderUI({       if (auth$authenticated) {         user_info <- auth$token@userinfo         tagList(           tags$p("You are logged in!"),           tags$pre(paste(capture.output(str(user_info)), collapse = "
"))         )       } else {         tags$p("You are not logged in.")       }     })   }    app_1 <- shinyApp(ui_1, server_1)   if (app_to_run == "1") {     runApp(       app_1,       port = 8100,       launch.browser = FALSE     )   }    # Example app with manual login button (2) -----------------------------------    ui_2 <- fluidPage(     use_shinyOAuth(),     actionButton("login_btn", "Login"),     uiOutput("login")   )    server_2 <- function(input, output, session) {     auth <- oauth_module_server(       "auth",       client,       auto_redirect = FALSE     )      observeEvent(input$login_btn, {       auth$request_login()     })      output$login <- renderUI({       if (auth$authenticated) {         user_info <- auth$token@userinfo         tagList(           tags$p("You are logged in!"),           tags$pre(paste(capture.output(str(user_info)), collapse = "
"))         )       } else {         tags$p("You are not logged in.")       }     })   }    app_2 <- shinyApp(ui_2, server_2)   if (app_to_run == "2") {     runApp(       app_2,       port = 8100,       launch.browser = FALSE     )   }    # Example app requesting additional resource with access token (3) -----------    # Below app shows the authenticated username + their GitHub repositories,   # fetched via GitHub API using the access token obtained during login    ui_3 <- fluidPage(     use_shinyOAuth(),     uiOutput("ui")   )    server_3 <- function(input, output, session) {     auth <- oauth_module_server(       "auth",       client,       auto_redirect = TRUE     )      repositories <- reactiveVal(NULL)      observe({       req(auth$authenticated)        # Example additional API request using the access token       # (e.g., fetch user repositories from GitHub)       resp <- perform_resource_req(         auth$token,         "https://api.github.com/user/repos"       )        if (httr2::resp_is_error(resp)) {         repositories(NULL)       } else {         repos_data <- httr2::resp_body_json(resp, simplifyVector = TRUE)         repositories(repos_data)       }     })      # Render username + their repositories     output$ui <- renderUI({       if (isTRUE(auth$authenticated)) {         user_info <- auth$token@userinfo         repos <- repositories()          return(tagList(           tags$p(paste("You are logged in as:", user_info$login)),           tags$h4("Your repositories:"),           if (!is.null(repos)) {             tags$ul(               Map(                 function(url, name) {                   tags$li(tags$a(href = url, target = "_blank", name))                 },                 repos$html_url,                 repos$full_name               )             )           } else {             tags$p("Loading repositories...")           }         ))       }        return(tags$p("You are not logged in."))     })   }    app_3 <- shinyApp(ui_3, server_3)   if (app_to_run == "3") {     runApp(       app_3,       port = 8100,       launch.browser = FALSE     )   } }

title: Wrap a Shiny UI to enable OAuth 2.0/OIDC form_post callbacks — oauth_form_post_ui
code: if (   # Example requires a local or remote Keycloak realm whose client allows   # http://127.0.0.1:8100/callback as a valid redirect URI.   nzchar(Sys.getenv("KEYCLOAK_BASE_URL")) &&     nzchar(Sys.getenv("KEYCLOAK_REALM")) &&     nzchar(Sys.getenv("KEYCLOAK_CLIENT_ID")) &&     interactive() ) {   library(shiny)   library(shinyOAuth)    provider <- oauth_provider_keycloak(     base_url = Sys.getenv("KEYCLOAK_BASE_URL"),     realm = Sys.getenv("KEYCLOAK_REALM")   )    client <- oauth_client(     provider = provider,     client_id = Sys.getenv("KEYCLOAK_CLIENT_ID"),     client_secret = Sys.getenv("KEYCLOAK_CLIENT_SECRET"),     redirect_uri = "http://127.0.0.1:8100/callback",     scopes = c("openid", "profile", "email"),     response_mode = "form_post"   )    base_ui <- fluidPage(     uiOutput("login")   )    ui <- oauth_form_post_ui(base_ui, id = "auth", client = client)    server <- function(input, output, session) {     auth <- oauth_module_server("auth", client, auto_redirect = TRUE)      output$login <- renderUI({       if (auth$authenticated) {         user_info <- auth$token@userinfo         tagList(           tags$p("You are logged in!"),           tags$pre(paste(capture.output(str(user_info)), collapse = "\n"))         )       } else {         tags$p("You are not logged in.")       }     })   }    runApp(     shinyApp(ui, server, uiPattern = ".*"),     port = 8100,     launch.browser = FALSE   ) }

title: OAuth 2.0 & OIDC authentication module for Shiny applications — oauth_module_server
text: function implements Shiny module server manages OAuth 2.0/OIDC authentication Shiny applications. handles OAuth 2.0/OIDC flow, including redirecting users authorization endpoint, securely processing callback, exchanging authorization codes tokens, verifying tokens, managing token refresh. also provides options automatic manual login flows, session expiry, proactive token refresh. Note: using module, must include shinyOAuth::use_shinyOAuth() UI definition load necessary JavaScript dependencies.

title: OAuth 2.0 & OIDC authentication module for Shiny applications — oauth_module_server
text: apps need decide whether login starts automatically, whether enable async mode, whether token refresh happen proactively. remaining arguments mainly deployments need tighter control session lifetime, logout behavior, browser cookie settings. Blocking vs. async behavior: async = FALSE (default), network operations like token exchange refresh performed main R thread. Transient errors retried package's internal req_with_retry() helper, currently uses Sys.sleep() backoff. Shiny, Sys.sleep() blocks event loop entire worker process, potentially freezing UI updates sessions worker slow provider responses retry backoff. keep UI responsive: set async = TRUE configure async backend runs main process, mirai daemons (mirai::daemons(n)) non-sequential future plan, reduce/block retries (see vignette("usage", package = "shinyOAuth")). Browser requirements: module relies browser's Web Crypto API generate secure, per-session browser token used state double-submit protection. Specifically, login flow requires window.crypto.getRandomValues available. present (example, old highly locked-browsers), module unable proceed authentication. case client-side error emitted surfaced server shinyOAuth_cookie_error containing message "webcrypto_unavailable". Use modern browser (enable Web Crypto) resolve . Browser cookie lifetime: opaque browser token cookie lifetime mirrors client's state_store TTL. Internally, module reads client@state_store$info()$max_age uses value cookie's Max-Age/Expires. cache expose finite max_age, conservative default 5 minutes (300 seconds) used align built-cachem::cache_mem(max_age = 300) default. Separately, state payload issued_at freshness window controlled client's state_payload_max_age (default 300 seconds).

if (   # Example requires configured GitHub OAuth 2.0 app   # (go to https://github.com/settings/developers to create one):   nzchar(Sys.getenv("GITHUB_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID")) &&     nzchar(Sys.getenv("GITHUB_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET")) &&     interactive() ) {   library(shiny)   library(shinyOAuth)    # Define client   client <- oauth_client(     provider = oauth_provider_github(),     client_id = Sys.getenv("GITHUB_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID"),     client_secret = Sys.getenv("GITHUB_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET"),     redirect_uri = "http://127.0.0.1:8100"   )    # Choose which app you want to run   app_to_run <- NULL   while (!isTRUE(app_to_run %in% c(1:4))) {     app_to_run <- readline(       prompt = paste0(         "Which example app do you want to run?
",         "  1: Auto-redirect login
",         "  2: Manual login button
",         "  3: Fetch additional resource with access token
",         "  4: No app (all will be defined but none run)
",         "Enter 1, 2, 3, or 4... "       )     )   }    if (app_to_run %in% c(1:3)) {     cli::cli_alert_info(paste0(       "Will run example app {app_to_run} on {.url http://127.0.0.1:8100}
",       "Open this URL in a regular browser (viewers in RStudio/Positron/etc. ",       "cannot perform necessary redirects)"     ))   }    # Example app with auto-redirect (1) -----------------------------------------    ui_1 <- fluidPage(     use_shinyOAuth(),     uiOutput("login")   )    server_1 <- function(input, output, session) {     # Auto-redirect (default):     auth <- oauth_module_server(       "auth",       client,       auto_redirect = TRUE     )      output$login <- renderUI({       if (auth$authenticated) {         user_info <- auth$token@userinfo         tagList(           tags$p("You are logged in!"),           tags$pre(paste(capture.output(str(user_info)), collapse = "
"))         )       } else {         tags$p("You are not logged in.")       }     })   }    app_1 <- shinyApp(ui_1, server_1)   if (app_to_run == "1") {     runApp(       app_1,       port = 8100,       launch.browser = FALSE     )   }    # Example app with manual login button (2) -----------------------------------    ui_2 <- fluidPage(     use_shinyOAuth(),     actionButton("login_btn", "Login"),     uiOutput("login")   )    server_2 <- function(input, output, session) {     auth <- oauth_module_server(       "auth",       client,       auto_redirect = FALSE     )      observeEvent(input$login_btn, {       auth$request_login()     })      output$login <- renderUI({       if (auth$authenticated) {         user_info <- auth$token@userinfo         tagList(           tags$p("You are logged in!"),           tags$pre(paste(capture.output(str(user_info)), collapse = "
"))         )       } else {         tags$p("You are not logged in.")       }     })   }    app_2 <- shinyApp(ui_2, server_2)   if (app_to_run == "2") {     runApp(       app_2,       port = 8100,       launch.browser = FALSE     )   }    # Example app requesting additional resource with access token (3) -----------    # Below app shows the authenticated username + their GitHub repositories,   # fetched via GitHub API using the access token obtained during login    ui_3 <- fluidPage(     use_shinyOAuth(),     uiOutput("ui")   )    server_3 <- function(input, output, session) {     auth <- oauth_module_server(       "auth",       client,       auto_redirect = TRUE     )      repositories <- reactiveVal(NULL)      observe({       req(auth$authenticated)        # Example additional API request using the access token       # (e.g., fetch user repositories from GitHub)       resp <- perform_resource_req(         auth$token,         "https://api.github.com/user/repos"       )        if (httr2::resp_is_error(resp)) {         repositories(NULL)       } else {         repos_data <- httr2::resp_body_json(resp, simplifyVector = TRUE)         repositories(repos_data)       }     })      # Render username + their repositories     output$ui <- renderUI({       if (isTRUE(auth$authenticated)) {         user_info <- auth$token@userinfo         repos <- repositories()          return(tagList(           tags$p(paste("You are logged in as:", user_info$login)),           tags$h4("Your repositories:"),           if (!is.null(repos)) {             tags$ul(               Map(                 function(url, name) {                   tags$li(tags$a(href = url, target = "_blank", name))                 },                 repos$html_url,                 repos$full_name               )             )           } else {             tags$p("Loading repositories...")           }         ))       }        return(tags$p("You are not logged in."))     })   }    app_3 <- shinyApp(ui_3, server_3)   if (app_to_run == "3") {     runApp(       app_3,       port = 8100,       launch.browser = FALSE     )   } }

if (   # Example requires configured Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) tenant:   nzchar(Sys.getenv("MS_TENANT")) && interactive() && requireNamespace("later") ) {   library(shiny)   library(shinyOAuth)    # Configure provider and client (Microsoft Entra ID with your tenant   client <- oauth_client(     provider = oauth_provider_microsoft(       # Provide your own tenant ID here (set as environment variable MS_TENANT)       tenant = Sys.getenv("MS_TENANT")     ),     # Default Azure CLI app ID (public client; activated in many tenants):     client_id = "04b07795-8ddb-461a-bbee-02f9e1bf7b46",     redirect_uri = "http://localhost:8100",     scopes = c("openid", "profile", "email")   )    # UI   ui <- fluidPage(     use_shinyOAuth(),     h3("OAuth demo (Microsoft Entra ID)"),     uiOutput("oauth_error"),     tags$hr(),     h4("Auth object (summary)"),     verbatimTextOutput("auth_print"),     tags$hr(),     h4("User info"),     verbatimTextOutput("user_info")   )    # Server   server <- function(input, output, session) {     auth <- oauth_module_server("auth", client)      output$auth_print <- renderText({       authenticated <- auth$authenticated       tok <- auth$token       err <- auth$error        paste0(         "Authenticated?",         if (isTRUE(authenticated)) " YES" else " NO",         "
",         "Has token? ",         if (!is.null(tok)) "YES" else "NO",         "
",         "Has error? ",         if (!is.null(err)) "YES" else "NO",         "

",         "Token present: ",         !is.null(tok),         "
",         "Has refresh token: ",         !is.null(tok) && isTRUE(nzchar(tok@refresh_token %||% "")),         "
",         "Has ID token: ",         !is.null(tok) && !is.na(tok@id_token),         "
",         "Expires at: ",         if (!is.null(tok)) tok@expires_at else "N/A"       )     })      output$user_info <- renderPrint({       req(auth$token)       auth$token@userinfo     })      observeEvent(       list(auth$error, auth$error_description),       {         if (interactive() && !is.null(auth$error_description)) {           rlang::inform(c(             "OAuth error details",             "i" = paste0("error: ", auth$error),             "i" = paste0("error_description: ", auth$error_description)           ))         }       },       ignoreInit = TRUE     )      output$oauth_error <- renderUI({       if (is.null(auth$error)) {         return(NULL)       }        msg <- if (identical(auth$error, "access_denied")) {         "Sign-in was canceled or denied. Please try again."       } else {         "Authentication failed. Please try again."       }        div(class = "alert alert-danger", role = "alert", msg)     })   }    # Need to open app in 'localhost:8100' to match with redirect_uri   # of the public Azure CLI app (above). Browser must use 'localhost'   # too to properly set the browser cookie. But Shiny only redirects to   # '127.0.0.1' & blocks process once it runs. So we disable browser   # launch by Shiny & then use 'later::later()' to open the browser   # ourselves a short moment after the app starts   later::later(     function() {       utils::browseURL("http://localhost:8100")     },     delay = 0.25   )    # Run app   runApp(shinyApp(ui, server), port = 8100, launch.browser = FALSE) }

title: Refresh an OAuth 2.0 token — refresh_token
code: # Please note: `get_userinfo()`, `introspect_token()`, and `refresh_token()` # are typically not called by users of this package directly, but are called # internally by `oauth_module_server()`. These functions are exported # nonetheless for advanced use cases. Most users will not need to # call these functions directly  # Example requires a real token from a completed OAuth flow # (code is therefore not run; would error with placeholder values below) if (interactive()) {   # Define client   client <- oauth_client(     provider = oauth_provider_github(),     client_id = Sys.getenv("GITHUB_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID"),     client_secret = Sys.getenv("GITHUB_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET"),     redirect_uri = "http://127.0.0.1:8100"   )    # Have a valid OAuthToken object; fake example below   # (typically provided by `oauth_module_server()` or `handle_callback()`)   token <- handle_callback(client, "<code>", "<payload>", "<browser_token>")    # Get userinfo   user_info <- get_userinfo(client, token)    # Introspect token (if supported by provider)   introspection <- introspect_token(client, token)    # Refresh token   new_token <- refresh_token(client, token, introspect = TRUE) }

Defaults "GET". effective token type DPoP, must final request method proof signed . headers Optional named list named character vector extra headers set request. Header names case-insensitive. user-supplied Authorization DPoP header ignored ensure token authentication set function overridden. query Optional named list query parameters append URL. follow_redirect Logical. FALSE (default), HTTP redirects disabled prevent leaking access token unexpected hosts. Set TRUE trust possible redirect targets understand security implications. check_url Logical. TRUE (default), validates url is_ok_host() attaching access token. rejects relative URLs, plain HTTP non-loopback hosts, options(shinyOAuth.allowed_hosts) set, hosts outside allowlist. Set FALSE already validated URL understand security implications. oauth_client Optional OAuthClient. Required effective token type DPoP, client carries configured DPoP proof key, also using sender-constrained mTLS / certificate-bound tokens shinyOAuth can attach configured client certificate validate cnf thumbprint OAuthToken observe cnf thumbprint carried raw JWT access-token string. token_type Optional override access token type token supplied raw string. Supported values Bearer DPoP. Invalid multi-valued inputs rejected. omitted, shinyOAuth preserves OAuthToken@token_type, may infer DPoP explicit OAuthToken@cnf$jkt metadata. Raw access-token strings default Bearer unless pass token_type = "DPoP" explicitly. dpop_nonce Optional DPoP nonce embed proof request. primarily useful resource server challenges DPoP-Nonce.

text: DPoP proofs bind current HTTP method target URI (without query fragment). Adding query parameters resource_req() fine, changing method, scheme, host, path invalidates proof.

title: Build an authenticated httr2 request for a protected resource — resource_req
code: # Make request using OAuthToken object # (code is not run because it requires a real token from user interaction) if (interactive()) {   # Get an OAuthToken   # (typically provided as reactive return value by `oauth_module_server()`)   token <- OAuthToken()    # Recommended for most callers: build + perform in one step.   response <- perform_resource_req(     token,     "https://api.example.com/resource",     query = list(limit = 5)   )    # Build only when you need to inspect the request yourself.   request <- resource_req(     token,     "https://api.example.com/resource",     query = list(limit = 5)   )    httr2::req_dry_run(request)    # Or start from your own httr2 request and still let shinyOAuth perform it   # so DPoP nonce retries remain available.   custom_request <- httr2::request("https://api.example.com/resource") |>     httr2::req_headers(Accept = "application/json") |>     httr2::req_url_query(limit = 5)    response <- perform_resource_req(token, custom_request) }

what: shinyOAuth: Provider-Agnostic OAuth Authentication for 'shiny' Applications — shinyOAuth-package
title: shinyOAuth: Provider-Agnostic OAuth Authentication for 'shiny' Applications — shinyOAuth-package
text: Provides simple, configurable, provider-agnostic 'OAuth 2.0' 'OpenID Connect' (OIDC) authentication framework 'shiny' applications using 'S7' classes. Defines providers, clients, tokens, well various supporting functions 'shiny' module. Features include cross-site request forgery (CSRF) protection, state encryption, 'Proof Key Code Exchange' (PKCE) handling, validation OIDC identity tokens (nonces, signatures, claims), automatic user info retrieval, asynchronous flows, hooks audit logging.

title: shinyOAuth (development version)
text: List-like OAuth/request/test payload access now consistently uses exact [[...]] indexing instead $, reducing accidental partial matches across runtime code, tests, integration fixtures. oauth_provider_oidc_discover() helpers built now accept discovery metadata differs configured issuer one trailing slash published issuer, still storing provider’s advertised issuer verbatim downstream iss checks. oauth_provider_okta() can now target Okta’s org authorization server auth_server = NULL, instead always forcing /oauth2/{auth_server} custom-server path. oauth_module_server() now warns client resolves response_mode = "form_post" prior oauth_form_post_ui() call detected module/client setup, helping catch missing form_post UI wrappers earlier. Added JWT Secured Authorization Response Mode (JARM) support response_mode = "jwt", "query.jwt", "form_post.jwt". oauth_provider_oidc_discover() now: Accepts either issuer base URL standard /.well-known/openid-configuration URL. Full discovery URLs normalized back issuer base request construction, strict issuer matching still applies without requiring issuer_match = "host". transport failures surface attempted /.well-known/openid-configuration URL plus underlying network error, making discovery misconfiguration connectivity problems easier diagnose. oauth_provider_apple() added configures Apple’s OIDC endpoints ID-token defaults, added oauth_client_secret_apple() generates ES256 JWT Apple expects client_secret field OAuthClient configured oauth_provider_apple(). OAuthClient printing now handles client_secret = "" cleanly public-client setups send secret, instead failing formatting redacted console preview.

title: shinyOAuth 0.5.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-05-23 Added mutual-TLS (‘mTLS’, RFC 8705) support, including mTLS client authentication, certificate-bound access tokens, mTLS endpoint aliases, exported oauth_client_mtls_registration() helper RFC 8705 client metadata. Added Demonstrating Proof--Possession (‘DPoP’, RFC 9449) support. Clients configured dpop_private_key now send DPoP proofs token protected-resource requests, include dpop_jkt authorization requests, can require token_type = "DPoP" plus cnf.jkt binding, replay one DPoP-Nonce challenge token protected-resource requests. Added JWT-Secured Authorization Request (‘JAR’, RFC 9101) support. oauth_client() can now send signed encrypted Request Objects via request_object_mode = "request" (sent parameter) via request_object_mode = "request_uri" (served Shiny app). Added Pushed Authorization Request (‘PAR’, RFC 9126) support. Providers can now configure par_url directly pick OIDC discovery, login flows push authorization request redirect returned request_uri. Added response_mode = "form_post" support authorization-code callbacks. Apps can wrap UI oauth_form_post_ui() Shiny accepts provider POST, stores callback server-side one-time handle, lets oauth_module_server() finish existing state, issuer, token exchange flow. Stored callback handles bounded effective state/store TTL shinyOAuth.callback_max_form_post_* size-cap options. Added OpenTelemetry (‘OTel’) support (using ‘otel’ package). ‘shinyOAuth’ now emits OTel logs existing audit events traces key OAuth operations module initialization, login/callback handling, token exchange/refresh, userinfo/introspection/revocation, session-end cleanup. See vignette("opentelemetry", package = "shinyOAuth") information. Observability audit logging improvements: Improved observability correlation existing audit flows. Interactive login now reuses single flow trace_id across redirect issuance, callback validation, token exchange, login outcome events, making easier correlate pre-redirect post-redirect Shiny sessions single login round-trip; async work also carries accurate originating Shiny session/process context worker-emitted events. audit_login_success$sub_source = "id_token" now reflects OAuthToken@id_token_validated result returned token, telemetry longer overstates ID-token validation tests debug options skip signature verification. Sanitized HTTP audit context now redacts remote_addr well proxy headers, default audit events longer export raw client IP addresses. Improved existing audit event types. audit_token_exchange audit_token_refresh now include expires_in_synthesized, indicating provider return usable expires_in shinyOAuth synthesize one; audit_login_failed now distinguishes async payload-validation state-store-lookup failures async token-exchange failures; audit_userinfo distinguishes missing sub JWT/JWKS validation failures; error-state consumption events use logical state digest available better correlation. See vignette("audit-logging", package = "shinyOAuth") information. options(shinyOAuth.trace_hook = ...) longer treated separate documented event sink. Prefer options(shinyOAuth.audit_hook = ...); old trace_hook option now remains backward-compatible alias audit_hook unset. Removed documented global shinyOAuth.print_errors / shinyOAuth.print_traceback options. Internal console error logging now uses explicit internal flags instead package-wide option fallbacks. http_error audit events now omit raw provider oauth_error_description text default keep oauth_error, oauth_error_uri, body_digest. raw description emitted options(shinyOAuth.expose_error_body = TRUE) enabled debugging. err_http() now strips query strings, fragments, userinfo response URLs surfacing condition messages emitted events, reducing leakage authorization codes, state, request URIs, similar URL-borne secrets. oauth_module_server() now: Explicitly ignores new login requests session already authenticated. Fails cleanly startup revoke_on_session_end = TRUE provider expose revocation_url, instead crashing formatting configuration error. Applies browser-token double-submit check OAuth error callbacks , deferring ?error=... handling browser token available treating browser-token mismatches invalid_state instead surfacing provider-controlled error text. Forwards oauth_client(introspect = TRUE) proactive refresh path, proactive refresh now follows refresh-time token introspection policy direct refresh_token(..., introspect = TRUE) calls. Preserves invalid_state callback error state CSRF/state/browser-token validation failures instead flattening paths token_exchange_error. Drops provider error_uri values unless absolute HTTPS URLs, unsafe schemes like javascript: longer surfaced values$error_uri. Validates browser_cookie_path strictly, requiring leading / rejecting semicolons control characters unsafe cookie attributes injected configured cookie Path. OAuthToken OAuthClient now print redacted token/secret/key previews instead exposing full credential material default console output. OAuthToken now tracks normalized granted_scopes plus granted_scopes_verified, apps can distinguish scope sets explicitly returned ones carried forward provider omitted scope. Refresh now preserves prior granted scopes instead widening back client’s configured scopes refresh response omits scope. oauth_client() (OAuthClient) now: Accepts custom state-store entries omit unused pkce_code_verifier nonce fields provider require , still rejecting missing PKCE nonce values checks enabled. Supports enforce_callback_issuer = TRUE require RFC 9207 iss callback parameter shared-redirect multi-issuer deployments. Relatedly, handle_callback() now accepts iss, advanced callers building around prepare_call() can supply callback issuer get client-level RFC 9207 check token exchange. Auto-enables RFC 9207 callback issuer enforcement caller leaves enforce_callback_issuer unset provider advertises authorization_response_iss_parameter_supported = TRUE. native RFC 8707 resource support, authorization, token exchange, refresh requests can request audience-restricted tokens without dropping manual extra params. Defaults scope_validation "warn", RFC-compliant reduced grants surface warnings unless opt scope_validation = "strict". Warns list-based OIDC claims requests use single-element values entry without (...), jsonlite::toJSON(auto_unbox = TRUE) otherwise serialize OIDC array constraint scalar. Rejects impossible JOSE alg/private-key combinations JWT client assertions DPoP proofs emitting invalid JOSE headers. Also enforces OIDC claim request value values constraints claims_validation enabled, just presence essential = TRUE claims. Defaults enforceable OIDC claim requests claims_validation = "warn" callers request essential value-constrained claims set claims_validation explicitly, claim mismatches surfaced default unless callers opt claims_validation = "none". Carries effective requested scopes whole login flow. openid auto-added authorization request, sealed state payload, token-response scope validation, introspection scope validation now use effective scope set. Uses validated ID token sub introspect_elements = "sub" falling back userinfo, unvalidated ID token payloads longer anchor introspection subject check. Binds sealed callback state effective provider client security policy used redirect. Multi-worker deployments must now keep callback/login validation settings aligned across workers; otherwise callbacks fail fast invalid_state instead resuming different worker policy. oauth_provider() (OAuthProvider) now: Allows response_mode = "query" response_mode = "form_post" authorization-code callbacks, still rejecting unsupported modes "fragment". provider metadata advertises response_modes_supported, shinyOAuth also fails fast explicit response mode advertised. Treats nonce-enabled OIDC flows sufficient validation context userinfo_id_token_match, shinyOAuth now always binds userinfo validated ID token subject baseline exists. Raises typed shinyOAuth_input_error conditions malformed constructor inputs vector endpoint URLs empty discovery-helper domains, apps can trap provider validation failures consistently. Validates custom jwks_cache$get() signatures without calling cache construction, avoiding side effects duck-typed cache backends. Validates allowed_algs shinyOAuth’s actual inbound verifier support longer accepts RSA-PSS (PS256, PS384, PS512) entries, previously present generic helper’s defaults despite lacking verifier support. Older configs explicitly allowed algorithms must switch supported verifier algorithms. Reserves refresh_token parameter name extra_token_params prevent duplicate refresh-token parameters token refresh requests. oauth_provider_oidc_discover() now: Fails fast metadata advertises PKCE methods omits S256. shinyOAuth keeps S256 default allows downgrade plain pass pkce_method = "plain" explicitly. Fails fast discovery document omits jwks_uri selected policy still needs signing keys, including id_token_validation = TRUE, nonce-enabled OIDC flows, signed UserInfo JWT validation. misconfigurations now fail provider setup instead later JWKS fetch. Validates discovered jwks_uri values absolute-URL, scheme, host policy used discovery endpoints, invalid disallowed JWKS URLs now fail discovery instead later first JWKS fetch. Rejects issuer inputs contain query strings fragments building discovery URL, matching stricter issuer validation already used manually configured providers. Rejects non-scalar endpoint metadata values configuration error. Honors jwks_host_allow_only early jwks_uri host check, explicitly pinned cross-host JWKS endpoints longer require disabling issuer-host matching. Maps token_endpoint_auth_methods_supported = ["none"] distinct public token auth style never sends client_secret, even oauth_client() picks one OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET. oauth_provider_oidc() now trims trailing slashes base_url deriving endpoint URLs configured issuer, avoiding valid ID tokens rejected strict OIDC iss comparison helper configured URL like https://issuer.example/. oauth_provider_microsoft() longer drops Microsoft alias tenants OAuth 2.0 plus userinfo identity default. common organizations now validate ID tokens using Microsoft’s tenant-independent issuer signing-key issuer rules, consumers now validates stable consumer tenant issuer. validate_id_token() now properly rejects auth_time claims set future (beyond leeway). Previously, future auth_time produced negative elapsed value always passed max_age freshness check. introspect_token() now uses provider@userinfo_id_selector consistently checks authenticated subject fetched UserInfo data, now fails closed malformed introspection JSON. Non-object responses rejected instead normalized first parsed element. refresh_token() now: Refuses update userinfo unless can verify refreshed identity new preserved ID token subject, preventing identity confusion providers omit id_token refresh responses. called introspect = TRUE, enforces token introspection hard policy check instead best-effort metadata enrichment. Refresh now fails introspection unsupported, inactive, malformed, missing required introspect_elements sub, client_id, scope. get_userinfo() now: Applies hard JWK alg compatibility checks signed UserInfo JWT verification ID token verification, rejecting JWKS keys advertise different algorithm even signature verification otherwise succeed. Always requires non-empty sub claim userinfo responses OIDC providers (issuer configured), per OIDC Core section 5.3. Previously, non-compliant response without sub accepted userinfo_id_token_match enabled. signed-JWT path (validate_signed_userinfo_claims()) also now checks sub alongside existing iss/aud validation. Signed UserInfo JWT validation now: Enforces exp, iat, nbf temporal claims present, rejecting expired -yet-valid UserInfo JWT responses instead accepting based signature/issuer/audience. oauth_client() can also require specific UserInfo JWT temporal claims present via userinfo_jwt_required_time_claims. Uses internal JWS verifier instead jose::jwt_decode_sig(), EdDSA UserInfo JWTs can verify correctly, provider leeway honored consistently, invalid typ headers rejected. Successful token refresh responses now always require token_type, even allowed_token_types = character(). empty allowlist still disables value allowlisting, longer waives RFC-required field. Refreshed OIDC ID tokens now enforce full continuity auth_time, refresh-time nonce, azp addition existing iss / sub / aud checks. Token exchange refresh requests longer retry transport errors transient HTTP statuses (408/429/5xx). Authorization codes single-use refresh tokens may rotated use; retrying server already committed first request replay invalidated credential, causing invalid_grant errors triggering refresh-token replay detection. Hardened runtime JWKS discovery validating discovery issuer trusting jwks_uri. policy now stored OAuthProvider via issuer_match, provider discovery runtime JWKS fetches apply rule: url exact issuer URL matching, host scheme--host matching, none skip discovery issuer check. JWKS caching now respects global host policy immediately. Cached entries scoped current allowed_hosts / allowed_non_https_hosts settings, cache hits re-check stored jwks_uri JWKS trusted. Scope validation now treats omitted scope initial token response unchanged requested scope, matching RFC 6749 section 5.1 instead rejecting otherwise compliant authorization servers default. Strict token-response introspection scope validation now treats commas part single scope token, matching RFC 6749 instead splitting scope = "read,write" separate read write scopes. Missing expires_in values now default finite 3600-second fallback rather effectively indefinite session. Override options(shinyOAuth.default_expires_in = <seconds>), use oauth_module_server(reauth_after_seconds = ...) need stricter session-age cap. err_http() now guards oversized HTTP error bodies hashing JSON parsing, large chunked misleading error responses now trip existing body-size limit consistently. at_hash validation now resolves EdDSA verified signing key/JWK instead guessing alg alone: Ed25519 uses exact SHA-512 mapping, signature-skipped currently unsupported EdDSA curves fail closed. Deprecated error_on_softened(). remains narrow guard dev/debug softeners, docs now stop presenting comprehensive deployment-hardening check show explicit option checks instead. Renamed resource-request helpers resource_req() perform_resource_req(). existing public client_bearer_req() name remains available deprecated alias, perform_client_bearer_req() also exported deprecated compatibility alias. perform_resource_req() new function builds performs authenticated resource-request , DPoP-bound access tokens, replays one use_dpop_nonce challenge server-provided nonce. can also take pre-existing ‘httr2’ request objects layer authentication DPoP top.

title: shinyOAuth 0.4.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-02-14 ‘mirai’ & async backend improvements: Warnings messages emitted async workers (e.g., missing expires_in token response) now captured re-emitted main process appear R console. includes conditions user-supplied trace_hook / audit_hook functions: warnings, messages, errors (surfaced warnings) propagate back main thread. Replay can disabled via options(shinyOAuth.replay_async_conditions = FALSE). Async callback flow longer serializes full client object (including potentially non-serializable custom state_store / JWKS cache backends) worker context. state_store (already consumed main thread) replaced lightweight serializable dummy dispatch. client still fails serialization, flow falls back synchronous execution explicit warning instead opaque runtime error. reduced serialization overhead towards async workers using certain functions package namespace directly. Detect active daemons via mirai::daemons_set() instead mirai::status(). Falls back mirai::info() older ‘mirai’ versions lack mirai::daemons_set() (< 2.3.0). Configurable per-task timeout via options(shinyOAuth.async_timeout) (milliseconds); timed-‘mirai’ tasks automatically cancelled dispatcher. Default NULL (timeout). Async audit events now include mirai_error_type field. classifies mirai transport-level failures separately application-level errors. Prevent ‘mirai’ warning spam ‘stats’ maybe available workers. ID token validation (validate_id_token()): Now enforces RFC 7515 section 4.1.11 critical header parameter (crit) processing rules. Tokens containing unsupported critical extensions rejected shinyOAuth_id_token_error. current implementation supports critical extensions, crit presence triggers rejection. Now validates at_hash (Access Token hash) claim present ID token (per OIDC Core section 3.1.3.8 3.2.2.9). claim exists, access token binding verified; mismatch raises shinyOAuth_id_token_error. New id_token_at_hash_required property OAuthProvider (default FALSE) forces login fail ID token contain at_hash claim. Now validates, refreshed ID tokens, per OIDC Core section 12.2, iss aud claims original ID token’s values (just provider configuration) cover edge cases multi-tenant providers rotating issuer URIs. Enforced validated non-validated code paths. Now detects encrypted ID tokens (JWE compact serialization, 5 dot-separated segments) early raises clear shinyOAuth_id_token_error instead letting confusing alg/typ/parse failure propagate. Now validates auth_time claim max_age present extra_auth_params (OIDC Core section 3.1.2.1). Now enforces maximum ID token lifetime (exp - iat) per OIDC Core section 3.1.3.7; tokens unreasonably long lifetimes rejected shinyOAuth_id_token_error. Configure via options(shinyOAuth.max_id_token_lifetime = <seconds>) (default 86400 24 hours). Set Inf disable check. Stricter state store usage: custom_cache() gains optional take parameter atomic get--delete. state_store_get_remove() prefers $take() available; falls back $get() + $remove() mandatory post-removal absence check (instead trusting $remove() return values). Non-cachem::cache_mem() stores without $take() now error default prevent TOCTOU replay attacks shared/multi-worker deployments. bypass error, operators must explicitly acknowledge risk setting options(shinyOAuth.allow_non_atomic_state_store = TRUE), downgrades error warning. OAuthClient validator now validates $take() signature present. $remove() return value longer relied upon fallback path; post-removal $get() absence check authoritative. Stricter JWKS cache handling: JWKS cache key now includes host-policy fields (jwks_host_issuer_match, jwks_host_allow_only). Previously, two provider configs issuer different host policies shared cache entry, allowing relaxed-policy provider populate cache strict-policy provider skip host validation cache hit. Cache entries now also store JWKS source host re-validate current provider policy read (defense--depth). Stricter URL validation: OAuthClient now rejects redirect URIs containing fragments (per RFC 6749, section 3.1.2); OAuthProvider now rejects issuer identifiers containing query fragment components, covering oauth_provider_oidc_discover() manual construction providers. Stricter state payload parsing: callback state now rejects embedded NUL bytes JSON decoding. Stricter response size validation: enforce max response body size outbound HTTP endpoints (token, introspection, userinfo, OIDC discovery, JWKS). Curl aborts transfer early Content-Length exceeds limit; post-download guard catches chunked responses. Default 1 MiB, configurable via options(shinyOAuth.max_body_bytes). OAuthProvider (S7 class): leeway validator now rejects non-finite values (Inf, -Inf, NaN). Previously passed validation silently coerced 0 runtime, effectively disabling clock-skew tolerance. Reserved OAuth parameter blocking extra_auth_params extra_token_params now case-insensitive trims whitespace. Vector inputs pkce_method URL parameters (auth_url, token_url, userinfo_url, introspection_url, revocation_url) now produce clear scalar-input errors instead cryptic coercion failures. OAuthClient (S7 class): Gains claims_validation property; client sends structured claims request parameter essential = TRUE entries, setting controls whether returned ID token /userinfo response checked essential claims (similar scope_validation). Gains required_acr_values property; enables client-side enforcement OIDC acr (Authentication Context Class Reference) claim. extra_token_headers now consistently applied revoke introspect requests, matching existing behavior token exchange refresh. Previously, provider integrations requiring custom headers across token endpoints partially fail revocation/introspection. Fixed incorrect warning client created Shiny case. Malformed client_assertion_alg client_assertion_audience values (e.g., character(0), multi-element vectors) now produce clear validation errors instead crashing base R subscript---bounds errors. Empty string "" client_assertion_audience now explicitly rejected instead silently treated “provided”. OAuthToken (S7 class): Gains read-id_token_claims property exposes decoded ID token JWT payload named list, surfacing OIDC claims (e.g., acr, amr, auth_time) without manual decoding. Gains id_token_validated property (logical) indicating whether ID token cryptographically verified OAuth flow. oauth_module_server(): Now surfaces error_uri provider error callbacks (RFC 6749, section 4.1.2.1). new $error_uri reactive field contains URI human-readable error page provider includes one; NULL otherwise. error_uri callback parameter also validated configurable size limit (e.g., options(shinyOAuth.callback_max_error_uri_bytes = 2048)). OAuth callback query cleanup now also applied early return paths internal function .process_query(), ensuring consistent cleanup. OAuth callback query size caps now enforced even user already authenticated. Previously, “token already present” branch .process_query() called .query_has_oauth_callback_keys() (parses query string) size validation, bypassing intended DoS guardrails. validate_untrusted_query_string() check now runs unconditionally top .process_query(). OAuth callback error responses (?error=...) now require valid state parameter. Missing/invalid/consumed state treated properly invalid_state error instead surfacing error ?error=... (set attacker). OAuth callback including iss query parameter now validate provider’s configured/discovered issuer callback processing (complementing existing ID token iss claim validation occurs post-exchange) (per RFC 9207). mismatch produces issuer_mismatch error audit event, defending authorization-server mix-attacks multi-provider scenarios. iss absent, current behavior retained (enforcement). handle_callback(): longer accepts decrypted_payload state_store_values bypass parameters. parameters intended internal use oauth_module_server()’s async path. can misused direct/custom callers bypass important security checks, moved internal-helper function (handle_callback_internal()). handle_callback()/refresh_token(): token response omits expires_in, warning now emitted per phase (exchange_code / refresh_token) operators know proactive token refresh trigger. Users can now also set finite default lifetime tokens via options(shinyOAuth.default_expires_in = <seconds>); unset, shinyOAuth now falls back 3600 seconds. get_userinfo() now supports JWT-encoded userinfo responses per OIDC Core, section 5.3.2. endpoint returns Content-Type: application/jwt, body decoded JWT. Verification fail-closed: signature verification always performed provider JWKS using provider’s allowed_algs, alg=none always rejected, unparseable headers, non-asymmetric algorithms, missing issuer/JWKS infrastructure raise errors. options(shinyOAuth.allow_unsigned_userinfo_jwt = TRUE) permits unsigned JWTs. New userinfo_signed_jwt_required property OAuthProvider (default FALSE) mandates userinfo endpoint returns application/jwt content-type subject verification. client_bearer_req() now validates target URL is_ok_host() attaching Bearer token. Relative URLs, plain HTTP non-loopback hosts, hosts outside options(shinyOAuth.allowed_hosts) rejected default. new check_url argument (default TRUE) allows opting check URL already validated. err_http() now extracts RFC 6749 section 5.2 structured error fields (error, error_description, error_uri) JSON error response bodies. fields surfaced error message bullets, attached condition object (oauth_error, oauth_error_description, oauth_error_uri), included trace/audit events. improves debugging token endpoint failures (e.g. invalid_grant, invalid_client) without changing existing control flow. OIDC claims parameter support (OIDC Core, section 5.5): OAuthClient oauth_client() now accept claims argument request specific claims userinfo Endpoint /ID token. Pass list structure (automatically JSON-encoded) pre-encoded JSON string. OIDC openid scope enforcement: provider issuer set (indicating OIDC) openid missing client’s scopes, build_auth_url() now auto-prepends emits one-time warning. OIDC discovery (oauth_provider_oidc_discover()) now prefers confidential auth methods (client_secret_basic, client_secret_post) none advertised token_endpoint_auth_methods_supported. Previously, mixed metadata (e.g. none + client_secret_basic) PKCE enabled silently select public-client posture ("body" without credentials). Scope validation now aligns RFC 6749, section 3.3 scope-token grammar (NQSCHAR = %x21 / %x23-5B / %x5D-7E). previous regex rejected valid ASCII characters !, #, $, =, @, ~, others. printable ASCII except space, double-quote, backslash now accepted. JWT helpers (build_client_assertion(), resolve_client_assertion_audience()) now defense--depth scalar guards malformed property values cause subscript errors runtime. Audit events: audit_token_refresh: replaced non-informative had_refresh_token field (always TRUE post-mutation) refresh_token_rotated (indicates whether provider returned new refresh token).

title: shinyOAuth 0.3.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-01-30 Async backend: default async backend now ‘mirai’ (>= 2.0.0) simpler efficient asynchronous execution. Use mirai::daemons() configure async workers. ‘future’ backend configured future::plan() still supported, ‘mirai’ takes precedence configured. Test suite: fixed inconsistent results several tests; tests suitable CRAN now skip CRAN. Silenced test output messages avoid confusion.

title: shinyOAuth 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-01-13

title: shinyOAuth 0.2.0
text: Token revocation: tokens can now revoked Shiny session ends. Enable via revoke_on_session_end = TRUE oauth_module_server(). provider must expose revocation_url (auto-discovered OIDC, set manually via oauth_provider()). New exported function revoke_token(). Token introspection login: validate tokens via provider’s introspection endpoint login. Configure via introspect introspect_elements properties OAuthClient. provider must expose introspection_url (auto-discovered OIDC, set manually via oauth_provider()). DoS protection: callback query parameters state payload/browser token sizes validated expensive operations (e.g., hashing audit logs). Maximum size may configured via options(); see section ‘Size caps’ vignette("usage", package = "shinyOAuth"). DoS protection: rate-limited JWKS refresh: forced JWKS cache refreshes (triggered unknown kid) now rate-limited prevent abuse. JWKS pinning: pinning now enforced signature verification: previously, jwks_pins jwks_pin_mode = "" verified least one key JWKS matched pin, signature verification still use matching key (pinned ). Now, signature verification restricted use keys whose thumbprints appear pin list, ensuring true key pinning rather presence-checks. use_shinyOAuth() now injects <meta name="referrer" content="-referrer"> default reduce leaking ?code=…&state=… via Referer header callback page. Can disabled use_shinyOAuth(inject_referrer_meta = FALSE). Sensitive outbound HTTP requests (token exchange/refresh, introspection, revocation, userinfo, OIDC discovery, JWKS) now default disable redirect following reject 3xx responses prevent bypassing host/HTTPS policies. Configurable via options(shinyOAuth.allow_redirect = TRUE). client_bearer_req() also gains follow_redirect, defaults FALSE, similarly control redirect behavior requests using bearer tokens. State now also consumed login failure paths (provider returns error also state). Callback URL parameters now also cleared login failure paths. OAuthProvider now requires absolute URLs (scheme + hostname) endpoint URLs. Provider fingerprint now includes userinfo_url introspection_url, reducing risk misconfiguration multiple providers share endpoints. state_payload_max_age property OAuthClient independent freshness validation state payload’s issued_at timestamp. Default client assertion JWT TTL reduced 5 minutes 120 seconds, reducing window replay attacks allowing clock skew.

title: shinyOAuth 0.2.0
text: New audit events: session_ended (logged Shiny session close), authenticated_changed (logged authentication status changes), token_introspection (introspect_token() used), token_revocation (revoke_token() used), error_state_consumed error_state_consumption_failed (called provider returns error callback handling state attempted consumed). audit events now include $process_id, $is_async, $main_process_id (called async worker); fields help identify process generated event whether async worker. Async workers now also properly propagate audit hooks main process (see ‘Fixed’). Audit event login_success now includes sub_source indicate whether subject digest came userinfo, id_token (verified), id_token_unverified. Audit digest keying: audit/event digests (e.g., sub_digest, browser_token_digest) now default HMAC-SHA256 auto-generated per-process key reduce reidentification/correlation risk logs leak. Configure key options(shinyOAuth.audit_digest_key = "..."), disable keying (legacy deterministic SHA-256) options(shinyOAuth.audit_digest_key = FALSE). HTTP log sanitization: sensitive data HTTP contexts (headers, cookies) now sanitized default audit logs. Can disabled options(shinyOAuth.audit_redact_http = FALSE). Use options(shinyOAuth.audit_include_http = FALSE) include HTTP data logs.

title: shinyOAuth 0.2.0
text: Configurable scope validation: validate_scopes property OAuthClient controls whether returned scopes validated requested scopes ("strict", "warn", "none"). Scopes now normalized (alphabetically sorted) comparison. OAuthProvider: extra parameters now blocked overriding reserved keys essential OAuth 2.0/OIDC flow. Reserved keys may explicitly overridden via options(shinyOAuth.unblock_auth_params = c(...), shinyOAuth.unblock_token_params = c(...), shinyOAuth.unblock_token_headers = c(...)). also validated early parameters named, catching configuration errors sooner. Added warning negative expires_in values token responses. Added warning OAuthClient instantiated inside Shiny session; may cause sealed state payload decryption fail random secret generated upon client creation. Added hints error messages sealed state payload decryption fails. Ensured clearer error message token response unexpected format. Ensured clearer error retrieved state store entry unexpected format. Ensured clearer error message retrieved userinfo parsed JSON. Immediate error OAuthProvider uses HS* algorithm options(shinyOAuth.allow_hs = TRUE) enabled; also immediate error OAuthProvider uses HS* algorithm ID token verification can happen client_secret absent weak. build_auth_url() now uses package-typed errors (err_invalid_state()) instead generic stopifnot() assertions, ensuring consistent error handling audit logging.

title: shinyOAuth 0.2.0
text: ID token signature/claims validation now occurs fetching userinfo. ensures cryptographic validation passes making external calls userinfo endpoint. fetching JWKS, key_ops present keys, keys key_ops including "verify" considered. oauth_provider() now defaults allowed_token_types c("Bearer") providers. prevents accidentally misusing non-Bearer tokens (e.g., DPoP, MAC) Bearer tokens. Set allowed_token_types = character() opt . Token type also now validated calling userinfo endpoint. client_assertion_audience property OAuthClient allows overriding JWT audience claim client assertion authentication.

title: shinyOAuth 0.2.0
text: Package now correctly requires httr2 >= 1.1.0. authenticated now flips FALSE promptly token expires reauth_after_seconds elapses, even without reactive changes. Previously, value remain TRUE past expiry unrelated reactive update triggered re-evaluation. HTTP error responses (4xx/5xx) now correctly returned caller immediately instead misclassified transport errors retried. Async worker options propagation: R options now automatically propagated async workers using async = TRUE. Previously, options set main process (including audit_hook, trace_hook, HTTP settings, custom options) available future::multisession workers. oauth_provider_microsoft(): fixed incorrect default blocked multi-tenant configuration. oauth_provider_oidc_discover(): stricter host matching; ? * wildcards now correctly handled. Fixed potential auto-redirect loop authentication error surfaced. Fixed potential race condition proactive refresh expiry watcher: expiry watcher now defers clearing token triggering reauthentication refresh progress. Token expiry handling token refresh now aligns handled login. State payload issued_at validation now applies clock drift leeway (OAuthProvider@leeway / shinyOAuth.leeway option), consistent ID token iat check.
